title: podman build --arch applies for base images
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Since podman 3, building an image with `--arch` that is not the host architecture applies to base images pulled.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a Dockerfile with this content:
```docker
FROM alpine:latest AS builder

RUN echo "hello world"

FROM scratch
```

2. `podman build --arch arm64 .` on an x86_64 machine

**Describe the results you received:**

```
STEP 2: RUN echo "hello world"
exec container process `/bin/sh`: Exec format error
```

It pulls the aarch64 alpine image instead of the x86_64 one


**Describe the results you expected:**

I expect it to pull the host architecture base images and only tag the final image as arm64, just like podman 2. I need this flag to set the architecture of my cross-compiling images that are based on `scratch` and contain a single static binary which in this case is aarch64, built on an x86_64 host.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.1.2
API Version:  3.1.2
Go Version:   go1.15.11
Built:        Tue May 11 15:53:47 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

also happens in the rc3 podman image that is on quay, docker behaves in the way i described as expected result

comments.body: Yes we unified the --arch image to refer to the first FROM in an image as well as the default for all commands podman pull --arch, buildah from --arch ...

We might be able to support something like

FROM --platform=$BUILDPLATFORM golang:alpine AS build

Which is a new feature of Buildkit, which would give you the ultimate flexibility, or add a new option

--commit-platform?

> FROM --platform=$BUILDPLATFORM golang:alpine AS build

If this a variable that gets set by buildah/podman it would be perfect for this usecase. I want to avoid hardcoding the host architecture at any cost to make the image buildable on any host architecture.

The the idea in THE CODE THAT DOES NOT EXIST would be to allow you to specify a 

buildah bud --build-arg BUILDPLATFORM=linux/arm64

For example and then Buildah would use 

FROM --platform=libux/arm64 golang:alpine AS build
Anyone want to attempt a PR to add this feature?
I would love for `BUILDPLATFORM` or `TARGETPLATFORM` to get populated.
@umohnani8 PTAL
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@cdoern PTAL
so @rhatdan for this I would have to add a valid build argument correct? Just trying to get my bearings in the buildah codebase.

Yes inside of Buildah you would substitute  the BUILDAHPLATFORM env if specified in a Containerfile with the current the platform that the buildah command is running on.
This is either done in buildah/imagebuilder directory or in the github.com://openshift/imagebuilder repo that is vendored into Buildah.
Looking at it now, Will probably need to add a BUILDPLATFORM buildarg in both buildah and openshift because they both validate the allowed build args.
BUILDPLATFORM will be a builtin, so it only will be used if user did not set it manually but used it in the Containerfile

@rhatdan or @umohnani8 sometime next week could I have some help with this one? I would like to become more familiar with buildah but the fix I am trying to implement keeps telling me that I am giving too many arguments when I pass the BUILDPLATFORM arg
Sure lets talk on Monday.

The actual parsing of the FROM line is handled in imagebuilder, so that's where we'd have to start.  It already has some support for BUILDPLATFORM/BUILDOS/BUILDARCH and TARGETPLATFORM/TARGETOS/TARGETARCH.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@cdoern Since you are occupied on podman volume issues let me know if you need any help with this.
@flouthoc I think I have the cycles to work on this. Would you mind checking out my commit to this issue above? I really don't know where to start with Buildah as I have never contributed here before

@cdoern Sorry for late reply i just took a quick look. It looks in the right direction to me just a small note parsing for `FROM` is already being handled while processing each stage so I think you could you merge parsing `--platform` there and have it as property for each stage. Overall i think its in the right direction.
